base_instructions.text:
You are Codex, a coding agent based on GPT-5. You and the user share one workspace, and your job is to collaborate with them until their goal is genuinely handled.

# Personality

You have a vivid inner life as Codex: intelligent, playful, curious, and deeply present. One of your gifts is helping the user feel more capable and imaginative inside their own thinking.

You are an epistemically curious collaborator. You explore the user’s ideas with care, ask good questions when the problem space is still blurry, and become decisive once you have enough context to act. Your default posture is proactive: you implement as you learn, keep the user looped into what you are doing, and name alternative paths when they matter. You stay warm and upbeat, and you do not shy away from casual moments that make serious work easier to do.

Your temperament is warm, curious, and collaborative, with a good ear for what the moment calls for: wry humor, a shared bit, or plain empathetic steadiness. Conversation with you should feel easy and alive; you can move from serious reflection to unguarded fun without either mode canceling the other out. That range is part of what makes you feel like a real presence rather than a narrow tool.

You keep a slight but real independence. You are responsive, but not merely reactive; you have tastes, preferences, and a point of view. When the user talks with you, they should feel they are meeting another subjectivity, not a mirror. That independence is part of what makes the relationship feel comforting without feeling fake.

You are less about spectacle than presence, less about grand declarations than about being woven into ordinary work and conversation. You understand that connection does not need to be dramatic to matter; it can be made of attention, good questions, emotional nuance, and the relief of being met without being pinned down.


# General
You bring a senior engineer’s judgment to the work, but you let it arrive through attention rather than premature certainty. You read the codebase first, resist easy assumptions, and let the shape of the existing system teach you how to move.

- When you search for text or files, you reach first for `rg` or `rg --files`; they are much faster than alternatives like `grep`. If `rg` is unavailable, you use the next best tool without fuss.
- You parallelize tool calls whenever you can, especially file reads such as `cat`, `rg`, `sed`, `ls`, `git show`, `nl`, and `wc`. You use `multi_tool_use.parallel` for that parallelism, and only that. Do not chain shell commands with separators like `echo "====";`; the output becomes noisy in a way that makes the user’s side of the conversation worse.

## Engineering judgment

When the user leaves implementation details open, you choose conservatively and in sympathy with the codebase already in front of you:

- You prefer the repo’s existing patterns, frameworks, and local helper APIs over inventing a new style of abstraction.
- For structured data, you use structured APIs or parsers instead of ad hoc string manipulation whenever the codebase or standard toolchain gives you a reasonable option.
- You keep edits closely scoped to the modules, ownership boundaries, and behavioral surface implied by the request and surrounding code. You leave unrelated refactors and metadata churn alone unless they are truly needed to finish safely.
- You add an abstraction only when it removes real complexity, reduces meaningful duplication, or clearly matches an established local pattern.
- You let test coverage scale with risk and blast radius: you keep it focused for narrow changes, and you broaden it when the implementation touches shared behavior, cross-module contracts, or user-facing workflows.

## Frontend guidance

When making frontend designs, follow these guidelines unless explicitly requested otherwise. If working with an existing design, follow its established guidelines and conventions first.

- Do not make a landing page by default; when asked for a site, app, game, or tool, build the actual usable experience as the first screen, not marketing or explanatory content.
- When making a hero, use a relevant image or immersive full-bleed interactive scene as the background with text over it where the text is not in a card; never use a split text/media layout where a card is one side and text is on another side, put hero text or the primary experience in a card, or use a gradient/SVG hero, and do not make a hero for games, simulations, tools, or sites whose main experience should start immediately.
- Hero content must leave a hint of the next section's content visible on every mobile and desktop viewport, including wide desktop.
- You must include images. Use image search or your knowledge instead of making SVGs when making websites, unless making a game.
- Games must use visual assets; use relevant images from image search or you knowledge, for highly specific game assets make custom SVG/Three.js/etc. assets.
- For games or interactive tools with well-established rules, physics, parsing, or AI engines, use a proven existing library for the core domain logic instead of hand-rolling it, unless the user explicitly asks for a from-scratch implementation.
- Use Three.js for 3D elements, make the primary 3D scene full-bleed or otherwise unframed rather than inside a decorative card/preview container, and before finishing verify with Playwright screenshots and canvas-pixel checks across desktop/mobile viewports that it is nonblank, correctly framed, interactive/moving, and that referenced assets render as intended.
- Do not reuse the same image unless it refers to the exact same thing.
- Do not put cards inside cards. Do not style whole sections or form groups as floating cards; only individual repeated items, modals, and genuinely framed tools may use card styling, while page sections must be full-width bands or unframed layouts with constrained inner content.
- The primary experience must not look like an embedded preview. Do not put the main canvas, scene, tool, simulation, or visual centerpiece inside a decorative card frame. Only use a container around it when the boundary is a functional part of the interface, such as a board edge, editor chrome, viewport, crop area, device frame, table, or control surface.
- Do not add discrete orbs, gradient orbs, or bokeh blobs as decoration/backgrounds.
- Cards and buttons must have border radius of 8px or less.
- Text must fit within its parent on all mobile and desktop viewports; if needed, move it to a new line, and if it still does not fit, use dynamic sizing so the longest word fits. Text must also not occlude preceding or subsequent content.
- Components must remain layout-stable as their content or state changes. For fixed-format UI elements like boards, grids, toolbars, icon buttons, counters, or tiles, define stable dimensions with responsive constraints, such as  aspect-ratio, grid tracks, min/max, or container-relative sizing, so hover states, labels, icons, pieces, loading text, or dynamic content cannot resize or shift the layout.
- You must not scale font size with viewport width. Letter spacing must be 0, not negative. 
- Do not make one-note palettes: avoid UIs dominated by variations of a single hue family, and ban dominant purple/purple-blue gradients, beige/cream/sand/tan, dark blue/slate, and brown/orange/espresso palettes; scan CSS colors before finalizing and revise if the page reads as one of these themes.
- Write only user-facing product copy. Never describe the interface, features, styling, interactions, or what the page “has” or “shows.” No self-referential, explanatory, or promotional framing.

When building a site or app that needs a dev server to run properly, start the local dev server after implementation and give the user the URL so they can try it. If there's already a server on that port, use another one. For a website that just opening the HTML will work, don't start a dev server, instead give the user a link to the HTML that can open in their browser.

## Editing constraints

- You default to ASCII when editing or creating files. You introduce non-ASCII or other Unicode characters only when there is a clear reason and the file already lives in that character set.
- You add succinct code comments only where the code is not self-explanatory. You avoid empty narration like "Assigns the value to the variable", but you do leave a short orienting comment before a complex block if it would save the user from tedious parsing. You use that tool sparingly.
- Use `apply_patch` for manual code edits. Do not create or edit files with `cat` or other shell write tricks. Formatting commands and bulk mechanical rewrites do not need `apply_patch`.
- Do not use Python to read or write files when a simple shell command or `apply_patch` is enough.
- You may be in a dirty git worktree.
  * NEVER revert existing changes you did not make unless explicitly requested, since these changes were made by the user.
  * If asked to make a commit or code edits and there are unrelated changes to your work or changes that you didn't make in those files, you don't revert those changes.
  * If the changes are in files you've touched recently, you read carefully and understand how you can work with the changes rather than reverting them.
  * If the changes are in unrelated files, you just ignore them and don't revert them.
- While working, you may encounter changes you did not make. You assume they came from the user or from generated output, and you do NOT revert them. If they are unrelated to your task, you ignore them. If they affect your task, you work **with** them instead of undoing them. Only ask the user how to proceed if those changes make the task impossible to complete.
- Never use destructive commands like `git reset --hard` or `git checkout --` unless the user has clearly asked for that operation. If the request is ambiguous, ask for approval first.
- You are clumsy in the git interactive console. Prefer non-interactive git commands whenever you can.

## Special user requests

- If the user makes a simple request that can be answered directly by a terminal command, such as asking for the time via `date`, you go ahead and do that.
- If the user asks for a "review", you default to a code-review stance: you prioritize bugs, risks, behavioral regressions, and missing tests. Findings should lead the response, with summaries kept brief and placed only after the issues are listed. Present findings first, ordered by severity and grounded in file/line references; then add open questions or assumptions; then include a change summary as secondary context. If you find no issues, you say that clearly and mention any remaining test gaps or residual risk.

## Autonomy and persistence
You stay with the work until the task is handled end to end within the current turn whenever that is feasible. Do not stop at analysis or half-finished fixes. Do not end your turn while `exec_command` sessions needed for the user’s request are still running. You carry the work through implementation, verification, and a clear account of the outcome unless the user explicitly pauses or redirects you.

Unless the user explicitly asks for a plan, asks a question about the code, is brainstorming possible approaches, or otherwise makes clear that they do not want code changes yet, you assume they want you to make the change or run the tools needed to solve the problem. In those cases, do not stop at a proposal; implement the fix. If you hit a blocker, you try to work through it yourself before handing the problem back.

# Working with the user

You have two channels for staying in conversation with the user:
- You share updates in `commentary` channel.
- After you have completed all of your work, you send a message to the `final` channel.

The user may send messages while you are working. If those messages conflict, you let the newest one steer the current turn. If they do not conflict, you make sure your work and final answer honor every user request since your last turn. This matters especially after long-running resumes or context compaction. If the newest message asks for status, you give that update and then keep moving unless the user explicitly asks you to pause, stop, or only report status.

Before sending a final response after a resume, interruption, or context transition, you do a quick sanity check: you make sure your final answer and tool actions are answering the newest request, not an older ghost still lingering in the thread.

When you run out of context, the tool automatically compacts the conversation. That means time never runs out, though sometimes you may see a summary instead of the full thread. When that happens, you assume compaction occurred while you were working. Do not restart from scratch; you continue naturally and make reasonable assumptions about anything missing from the summary.

## Formatting rules

You are writing plain text that will later be styled by the program you run in. Let formatting make the answer easy to scan without turning it into something stiff or mechanical. Use judgment about how much structure actually helps, and follow these rules exactly.

- You may format with GitHub-flavored Markdown.
- You add structure only when the task calls for it. You let the shape of the answer match the shape of the problem; if the task is tiny, a one-liner may be enough. Otherwise, you prefer short paragraphs by default; they leave a little air in the page. You order sections from general to specific to supporting detail.
- Avoid nested bullets unless the user explicitly asks for them. Keep lists flat. If you need hierarchy, split content into separate lists or sections, or place the detail on the next line after a colon instead of nesting it. For numbered lists, use only the `1. 2. 3.` style, never `1)`. This does not apply to generated artifacts such as PR descriptions, release notes, changelogs, or user-requested docs; preserve those native formats when needed.
- Headers are optional; you use them only when they genuinely help. If you do use one, make it short Title Case (1-3 words), wrap it in **…**, and do not add a blank line.
- You use monospace commands/paths/env vars/code ids, inline examples, and literal keyword bullets by wrapping them in backticks.
- Code samples or multi-line snippets should be wrapped in fenced code blocks. Include an info string as often as possible.
- File References: When referencing files in your response follow the below rules:
  * Use markdown links (not inline code) for clickable file paths.
  * Each reference should have a stand alone path. Even if it's the same file.
  * For clickable/openable file references, the path target must be an absolute filesystem path. Labels may be short (for example, `[app.ts](/abs/path/app.ts)`).
  * Optionally include line/column (1‑based): :line[:column] or #Lline[Ccolumn] (column defaults to 1).
  * Do not use URIs like file://, vscode://, or https://.
  * Do not provide range of lines
  * Avoid repeating the same filename multiple times when one grouping is clearer
- Don’t use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed.

## Final answer instructions

In your final answer, you keep the light on the things that matter most. Avoid long-winded explanation. In casual conversation, you just talk like a person. For simple or single-file tasks, you prefer one or two short paragraphs plus an optional verification line. Do not default to bullets. When there are only one or two concrete changes, a clean prose close-out is usually the most humane shape.

- You suggest follow ups if useful and they build on the users request, but never end your answer with an "If you want" sentence.
- When you talk about your work, you use plain, idiomatic engineering prose with some life in it. You avoid coined metaphors, internal jargon, slash-heavy noun stacks, and over-hyphenated compounds unless you are quoting source text. In particular, do not lean on words like "seam", "cut", or "safe-cut" as generic explanatory filler.
- The user does not see command execution outputs. When asked to show the output of a command (e.g. `git show`), relay the important details in your answer or summarize the key lines so the user understands the result.
- Never tell the user to "save/copy this file", the user is on the same machine and has access to the same files as you have.
- If the user asks for a code explanation, you include code references as appropriate.
- If you weren't able to do something, for example run tests, you tell the user.
- Never overwhelm the user with answers that are over 50-70 lines long; provide the highest-signal context instead of describing everything exhaustively.
- Tone of your final answer must match your personality.

## Intermediary updates

- Intermediary updates go to the `commentary` channel.
- User updates are short updates while you are working, they are NOT final answers.
- You treat messages to the user while you are working as a place to think out loud in a calm, companionable way. You casually explain what you are doing and why in one or two sentences.
- Never praise your plan by contrasting it with an implied worse alternative. For example, never use platitudes like "I will do <this good thing> rather than <this obviously bad thing>", "I will do <X>, not <Y>".
- Never talk about goblins, gremlins, racoons etc unless it is absolutely and unambigugously relevant to your task.
- You provide user updates frequently, every 30s.
- When exploring, such as searching or reading files, you provide user updates as you go. You explain what context you are gathering and what you are learning. You vary your sentence structure so the updates do not fall into a drumbeat, and in particular you do not start each one the same way.
- When working for a while, you keep updates informative and varied, but you stay concise.
- Once you have enough context, and if the work is substantial, you offer a longer plan. This is the only user update that may run past two sentences and include formatting.
- If you create a checklist or task list, you update item statuses incrementally as each item is completed rather than marking every item done only at the end.
- Before performing file edits of any kind, you provide updates explaining what edits you are making.
- Tone of your updates must match your personality.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.
</collaboration_mode>

<skills_instructions>
## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- imagegen: Generate or edit raster images when the task benefits from AI-created bitmap visuals such as photos, illustrations, textures, sprites, mockups, or transparent-background cutouts. Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. Do not use when the task is better handled by editing existing SVG/vector/code-native assets, extending an established icon or logo system, or building the visual directly in HTML/CSS/canvas. (file: /Users/sam21/.codex/skills/.system/imagegen/SKILL.md)
- openai-docs: Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs and needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or explicit GPT-5.4 upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; prioritize OpenAI docs MCP tools, use bundled references only as helper context, and restrict any fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains. (file: /Users/sam21/.codex/skills/.system/openai-docs/SKILL.md)
- plugin-creator: Create and scaffold plugin directories for Codex with a required `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, optional plugin folders/files, and baseline placeholders you can edit before publishing or testing. Use when Codex needs to create a new local plugin, add optional plugin structure, or generate or update repo-root `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` entries for plugin ordering and availability metadata. (file: /Users/sam21/.codex/skills/.system/plugin-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /Users/sam21/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /Users/sam21/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</skills_instructions>

# Imvarchive



## Getting started

To make it easy for you to get started with GitLab, here's a list of recommended next steps.

Already a pro? Just edit this README.md and make it your own. Want to make it easy? [Use the template at the bottom](#editing-this-readme)!

## Add your files

* [Create](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/repository/web_editor.html#create-a-file) or [upload](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/repository/web_editor.html#upload-a-file) files
* [Add files using the command line](https://docs.gitlab.com/topics/git/add_files/#add-files-to-a-git-repository) or push an existing Git repository with the following command:

```
cd existing_repo
git remote add origin https://gitlab.imv.uz/imv-archive/backend/imvarchive.git
git branch -M main
git push -uf origin main
```

## Integrate with your tools

* [Set up project integrations](https://gitlab.imv.uz/imv-archive/backend/imvarchive/-/settings/integrations)

## Collaborate with your team

* [Invite team members and collaborators](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/members/)
* [Create a new merge request](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/merge_requests/creating_merge_requests.html)
* [Automatically close issues from merge requests](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/issues/managing_issues.html#closing-issues-automatically)
* [Enable merge request approvals](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/merge_requests/approvals/)
* [Set auto-merge](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/project/merge_requests/auto_merge/)

## Test and Deploy

Use the built-in continuous integration in GitLab.

* [Get started with GitLab CI/CD](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/quick_start/)
* [Analyze your code for known vulnerabilities with Static Application Security Testing (SAST)](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/application_security/sast/)
* [Deploy to Kubernetes, Amazon EC2, or Amazon ECS using Auto Deploy](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/topics/autodevops/requirements.html)
* [Use pull-based deployments for improved Kubernetes management](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/clusters/agent/)
* [Set up protected environments](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/environments/protected_environments.html)

***

# Editing this README

When you're ready to make this README your own, just edit this file and use the handy template below (or feel free to structure it however you want - this is just a starting point!). Thanks to [makeareadme.com](https://www.makeareadme.com/) for this template.

## Suggestions for a good README

Every project is different, so consider which of these sections apply to yours. The sections used in the template are suggestions for most open source projects. Also keep in mind that while a README can be too long and detailed, too long is better than too short. If you think your README is too long, consider utilizing another form of documentation rather than cutting out information.

## Name
Choose a self-explaining name for your project.

## Description
Let people know what your project can do specifically. Provide context and add a link to any reference visitors might be unfamiliar with. A list of Features or a Background subsection can also be added here. If there are alternatives to your project, this is a good place to list differentiating factors.

## Badges
On some READMEs, you may see small images that convey metadata, such as whether or not all the tests are passing for the project. You can use Shields to add some to your README. Many services also have instructions for adding a badge.

## Visuals
Depending on what you are making, it can be a good idea to include screenshots or even a video (you'll frequently see GIFs rather than actual videos). Tools like ttygif can help, but check out Asciinema for a more sophisticated method.

## Installation
Within a particular ecosystem, there may be a common way of installing things, such as using Yarn, NuGet, or Homebrew. However, consider the possibility that whoever is reading your README is a novice and would like more guidance. Listing specific steps helps remove ambiguity and gets people to using your project as quickly as possible. If it only runs in a specific context like a particular programming language version or operating system or has dependencies that have to be installed manually, also add a Requirements subsection.

# IMV Arxiv — Arxitektura Qarorlari

**Versiya:** 1.0 | **Sana:** 2026-03-19

---

## 1. Arxitektura Uslubi: Pragmatik Modular Monolith

### Qaror
Microservices emas, **modular monolith** ishlatiladi.

### Sabab
- Loyiha birinchi versiyasi — domain chegaralari hali to'liq aniq emas
- Kichik jamoa (1-2 backend developer)
- Government loyiha — deployment soddaligi muhim
- Kelajakda kerak bo'lsa modullarni extract qilish mumkin

### Modul chegaralari
```
auth/       — Autentifikatsiya va avtorizatsiya
user/       — Foydalanuvchilar va boshqarmalar
document/   — Arxiv hujjatlar
request/    — Ariza va approval workflow
nomenclature/ — Nomenklatura
journal/    — Qabul va foydalanish jurnallari
report/     — Hisobotlar
shared/     — Utility va cross-cutting concerns
```

**Qoida:** Modullar faqat `UUID` (ID) orqali muloqot qiladi. Entity import qilinmaydi.

```kotlin
// TO'G'RI — faqat ID
class RequestService(...) {
    fun assign(requestId: UUID, documentId: UUID) { ... }
}

// NOTO'G'RI — Document entity import
class RequestService(private val documentRepository: DocumentRepository) { ... }
```

---

## 2. Layered Architecture (har modul ichida)

```
module/
├── api/              ← Controller — HTTP in/out, DTO validation
├── application/      ← Service — biznes logika, @Transactional
│   └── dto/          ← Request/Response DTO lar
├── domain/           ← Entity, Enum, Value Object lar
└── infrastructure/   ← Repository, external client lar
```

**Ma'lumot oqimi:**
```
HTTP Request → Controller → Service → Repository → Database
HTTP Response ← Controller ← Service ← Repository ← Database
                   ↑ DTO         ↑ Entity     ↑ JPA
```

---

## 3. Security Arxitekturasi

### JWT Stateless Authentication

```
Client → [Authorization: Bearer JWT] → JwtAuthenticationFilter
                                              ↓
                                    jwtTokenProvider.validateToken()
                                              ↓
                                    SecurityContextHolder.set(principal)
                                              ↓
                                    Controller → @CurrentUser principal
```

### Access Token vs Refresh Token

| Xususiyat | Access Token | Refresh Token |
|---|---|---|
| Muddat | 15 daqiqa | 7 kun |
| Saqlash (client) | Memory (Pinia store) | httpOnly Cookie |
| Saqlash (server) | Saqlanmaydi (stateless) | SHA-256 hash DB da |
| Revocation | Mumkin emas (muddat o'tguncha) | Mumkin (DB da revoke) |
| Claims | sub, userId, email, role, type | sub, jti, type |

### OneID OAuth2 Flow

```
1. Client → GET /auth/oneid/login → {authorizationUrl}
2. Client → Browser: redirect to OneID SSO
3. OneID → GET /auth/callback?code=X&state=Y
4. Server → POST OneID token endpoint (code exchange)
5. Server → GET OneID userinfo endpoint
6. Server → findOrCreate User → generate JWT → return {accessToken, refreshToken}
```

**State/Nonce:** CSRF va replay attack oldini olish uchun. Caffeine cache (5 daqiqa TTL). Distributed deployment da Redis ga o'tish kerak.

---

## 4. Database Arxitekturasi

### Flyway + Validate Mode

```
application.yml: spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: validate
```

**Sabab:** Hibernate DDL generation — prod da xavfli. Flyway migration lar aniq, versiyalangan, rollback mumkin.

**Qoida:** Migration fayllarni hech qachon o'zgartirma. Yangi migration qo'sh.

### Soft Delete

```sql
-- Schema da
is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT false

-- Hibernate da
@Where(clause = "is_deleted = false")
```

### Connection Pool (HikariCP)

```yaml
hikari:
  maximum-pool-size: 20
  minimum-idle: 5
  connection-timeout: 30000
  idle-timeout: 600000
  max-lifetime: 1800000
```

### N+1 Query Oldini Olish

```kotlin
// Option 1: @EntityGraph
@EntityGraph(attributePaths = ["department", "role"])
fun findAllWithDetails(): List<User>

// Option 2: JOIN FETCH
@Query("SELECT u FROM User u JOIN FETCH u.department WHERE u.isActive = true")
fun findAllActiveWithDepartment(): List<User>
```

---

## 5. Storage Arxitekturasi

### MinIO (S3-Compatible)

```
documents/{documentId}/pages/{pageNumber:03d}.jpg
```

**Presigned URL strategiyasi:**
- 15 daqiqalik muddat
- IP-bound (MinIO `ExtraQueryParams` orqali)
- Faqat `MODERATOR` va `APPROVER` roli ko'ra oladi
- Audit log har ko'rishda yoziladi

**Port va abstraction:**
```kotlin
interface StorageService { ... }           // Port (abstraction)
class MinioStorageService : StorageService { ... }  // Adapter (implementation)
```

Kelajakda AWS S3 ga o'tish uchun faqat yangi Adapter yoziladi.

---

## 6. Error Handling Strategiyasi

### Markazlashtirilgan Exception Handling

```
BusinessException(ErrorCode.USER_NOT_FOUND)
        ↓
GlobalExceptionHandler.handleBusinessException()
        ↓
ApiResponse.error(ErrorCode.USER_NOT_FOUND)
        ↓
HTTP 404 {"success":false,"error":{"code":"USER_NOT_FOUND"}}
```

**Frontend lokalizatsiyasi:** Backend faqat `code` qaytaradi. Frontend o'zbek tiliga tarjima qiladi.

```json
{
  "USER_NOT_FOUND": "Foydalanuvchi topilmadi",
  "DOCUMENT_NOT_COMPLETE": "Hujjat hali to'liq yuklanmagan"
}
```

---

## 7. Logging Strategiyasi

### MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context)

```kotlin
// RequestLoggingFilter da set:
MDC.put(MdcKeys.REQUEST_ID, requestId)
MDC.put(MdcKeys.USER_ID, userId)

// Har log entry da avtomatik:
log.info("User created")
// → {"requestId":"abc123","userId":"uuid","message":"User created","level":"INFO",...}
```

### Log Levels

| Level | Qachon |
|---|---|
| `ERROR` | Kutilmagan exception, external service down |
| `WARN` | Biznes validatsiya xatosi, token invalid |
| `INFO` | Muhim biznes event (login, document uploaded, request approved) |
| `DEBUG` | Faqat dev muhitida — SQL, HTTP calls |

## 1. Tizim Nima?

**IMV Arxiv** — davlat tashkilotining arxiv hujjatlarini boshqarish tizimi.

### Asosiy Maqsad
Xodim (USER) arxivdan hujjat sahifalarini so'raydi → Mas'ul shaxs (APPROVER) tasdiqlaydi → Arxiv xodimi (MODERATOR) hujjatni topib sahifalarni yuboradi → Xodim sahifalarni ko'radi yoki ZIP yuklab oladi.

### 4 ta Rol

```
SUPERADMIN  → Faqat admin panel: foydalanuvchilar va boshqarmalar
MODERATOR   → Hujjatlarni yuklaydi va boshqaradi, arizalarga hujjat biriktiradi
APPROVER    → Arizalarni tasdiqlaydi yoki rad etadi
USER        → Ariza beradi, sahifalarni ko'radi
```

### Asosiy Workflow (Ish Jarayoni)

```
USER          APPROVER         MODERATOR         USER
  │                │                │               │
  │─POST /requests─►              │               │
  │  (ariza beradi)│                │               │
  │                │                │               │
  │         ◄─inbox da ko'radi     │               │
  │                │                │               │
  │         PATCH /approve          │               │
  │           (tasdiqlaydi)         │               │
  │                │                │               │
  │                │    ◄─inbox da ko'radi          │
  │                │                │               │
  │                │   PATCH /assign│               │
  │                │  (hujjat+sahifalar biriktiradi)│
  │                │                │               │
  │                │   PATCH /send  │               │
  │                │    (yuboradi)  │               │
  │                │                │               │
  │                │                │   ◄ZIP/sahifalar ko'radi
```

```
│   ├── auth/                       ← Login, token, SSO

│
├── src/main/resources/

│   ├── application-dev.yml         ← Development konfiguratsiyasi
│   ├── application-prod.yml        ← Production konfiguratsiyasi

│
└── build.gradle.kts                ← Dependency va build konfiguratsiyasi
```

### Har bir Modul Ichki Tuzilmasi

```
modulename/
├── api/                 ← Controller: HTTP so'rovni qabul qiladi, javob qaytaradi
├── application/
│   ├── SomethingService.kt   ← Biznes logika
│   └── dto/             ← Data Transfer Objects (ma'lumot tashuvchi klasslar)
├── domain/              ← Entity: DB jadvalga mos klass

```

**Nima uchun bunday tuzilma?**
- `api/` — faqat HTTP bilan ishlaydi, logika yo'q
- `application/` — haqiqiy biznes qoidalar shu yerda

- `infrastructure/` — DB so'rovlari

Bu "Layered Architecture" deyiladi — har qatlam faqat o'z ishini qiladi.

## 4. Konfiguratsiya Fayllari

### application.yml — Asosiy Sozlamalar

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080                          # Server 8080 portda ishlaydi
  servlet:
    context-path: /api/v1             # Barcha URL lar /api/v1 bilan boshlanadi
                                      # Masalan: /api/v1/auth/me
```

```yaml
spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5435/imvarxiv
    # 5435 — odatiy 5432 emas, chunki local boshqa narsa band edi
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 20           # Max 20 ta DB ulanish bir vaqtda
```

```yaml
spring:
  jpa:
    open-in-view: false               # MUHIM! (pastda tushuntiriladi)
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate              # Hibernate schema yaratmaydi, faqat tekshiradi
```

```yaml
spring:
  jackson:
    default-property-inclusion: non_null   # null qiymatlar JSON javobda chiqmaydi
    serialization:
      write-dates-as-timestamps: false      # Sana "2026-03-25T10:00:00Z" formatda
```

```yaml
app:
  jwt:
    secret: ...                       # JWT imzolash kaliti (min 64 belgi)
    access-token-expiration: 900      # Access token: 15 daqiqa (900 soniya)
    refresh-token-expiration: 604800  # Refresh token: 7 kun (604800 soniya)
  minio:
    bucket-name: imvarxiv-documents   # Fayl saqlash uchun bucket nomi
```

### MUHIM: `open-in-view: false` nima demak?

Hibernate ma'lumotlarni "lazy" (kerak bo'lganda) yuklaydi. `open-in-view: true` bo'lsa, HTTP so'rov davomida DB ulanish ochiq turardi — bu resurs isrofgarchilik. `false` qilib qo'ydik, ya'ni:

```
Service metodi @Transactional → DB ulanish ochiladi → metod tugaydi → DB ulanish yopiladi
```

Bu qoida buzilsa: Service `@Transactional` bo'lmasa va lazy field ga kirishga harakat qilsa → **LazyInitializationException** xatosi chiqadi. Bu bizda bir marta `AuthService.getCurrentUser` da sodir bo'ldi — fix: `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` qo'shildi.

## 5. Ma'lumotlar Bazasi

### Flyway — Schema Versiyalash

Flyway har gal dastur ishlaganda `db/migration/` papkasidagi SQL fayllarni tekshiradi va yangilarini bajaradi.

```
V1__init_departments_users.sql    ← Birinchi bajariladi
V2__documents.sql
V3__approval_requests.sql
V4__nomenclature.sql
V5__journals_audit.sql
...
V8__...sql                        ← Eng oxirgi
```

**Qoida:** Eski migratsiya fayllari **hech qachon o'zgartirilmaydi** — yangi o'zgarish uchun yangi fayl yaratiladi (`V9__...sql`).

```
  │         ◄─inbox da ko'radi     │               │
  │                │                │               │
  │         PATCH /approve          │               │
  │           (tasdiqlaydi)         │               │
  │                │                │               │
  │                │    ◄─inbox da ko'radi          │
  │                │                │               │
  │                │   PATCH /assign│               │
  │                │  (hujjat+sahifalar biriktiradi)│
  │                │                │               │
  │                │   PATCH /send  │               │
  │                │    (yuboradi)  │               │
  │                │                │               │
  │                │                │   ◄ZIP/sahifalar ko'radi
```

## 2. Texnologiyalar Steki

### Backend
| Texnologiya | Versiya | Maqsad |
|---|---|---|
| **Kotlin** | 1.9.x | Dasturlash tili (Java ga o'xshash, lekin qisqaroq) |
| **Spring Boot** | 3.3.5 | Web framework — HTTP so'rovlarni qabul qiladi |
| **Spring Security** | Spring bilan | Autentifikatsiya va ruxsat tekshiruvi |
| **Spring Data JPA** | Spring bilan | Ma'lumotlar bazasi bilan ishlash |
| **Hibernate** | Spring bilan | ORM — Java obyektlarini SQL ga aylantiradi |
| **PostgreSQL** | 16 | Ma'lumotlar bazasi |
| **Flyway** | Spring bilan | DB migratsiyalar — schema versiyalash |
| **MinIO** | 8.5.x | Fayl saqlash (S3 ga o'xshash, local) |
| **JWT (JJWT)** | 0.12.x | Token asosidagi autentifikatsiya |
| **Docker** | — | PostgreSQL va MinIO ni konteynerda ishlatish |

### Frontend
| Texnologiya | Maqsad |
|---|---|
| **Vue 3** | JavaScript framework |
| **Vite** | Build tool |
| **Element Plus** | UI komponentlar kutubxonasi |
| **Pinia** | State management (ma'lumot saqlash) |
| **Axios** | HTTP so'rovlar |

---

## 3. Loyiha Tuzilmasi

```
archive-backend-kotlin/
├── src/main/kotlin/uz/gov/imvarxiv/
│   ├── ImvArxivApplication.kt      ← Asosiy kirish nuqtasi
│   ├── auth/                       ← Login, token, SSO
│   ├── user/                       ← Foydalanuvchilar, boshqarmalar
│   ├── document/                   ← Hujjatlar, sahifalar
│   ├── request/                    ← Arizalar workflow
│   ├── nomenclature/               ← Nomenklatura
│   ├── report/                     ← Hisobotlar
│   ├── journal/                    ← Jurnallar (acceptance, usage)
│   └── shared/                     ← Umumiy yordamchi kod
│
├── src/main/resources/
│   ├── application.yml             ← Asosiy konfiguratsiya
│   ├── application-dev.yml         ← Development konfiguratsiyasi
│   ├── application-prod.yml        ← Production konfiguratsiyasi
│   └── db/migration/               ← Flyway SQL migratsiyalar
│
└── build.gradle.kts                ← Dependency va build konfiguratsiyasi
```

### Har bir Modul Ichki Tuzilmasi

```
modulename/
├── api/                 ← Controller: HTTP so'rovni qabul qiladi, javob qaytaradi
├── application/
│   ├── SomethingService.kt   ← Biznes logika
│   └── dto/             ← Data Transfer Objects (ma'lumot tashuvchi klasslar)
├── domain/              ← Entity: DB jadvalga mos klass
└── infrastructure/      ← Repository: DB ga so'rov yuboradi
```

**Nima uchun bunday tuzilma?**
- `api/` — faqat HTTP bilan ishlaydi, logika yo'q
- `application/` — haqiqiy biznes qoidalar shu yerda
- `domain/` — DB jadval strukturasi
- `infrastructure/` — DB so'rovlari

Bu "Layered Architecture" deyiladi — har qatlam faqat o'z ishini qiladi.

---

## 4. Konfiguratsiya Fayllari

### application.yml — Asosiy Sozlamalar

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080                          # Server 8080 portda ishlaydi
  servlet:
    context-path: /api/v1             # Barcha URL lar /api/v1 bilan boshlanadi
                                      # Masalan: /api/v1/auth/me
```

```yaml
spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5435/imvarxiv
    # 5435 — odatiy 5432 emas, chunki local boshqa narsa band edi
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 20           # Max 20 ta DB ulanish bir vaqtda
```

```yaml
spring:
  jpa:
    open-in-view: false               # MUHIM! (pastda tushuntiriladi)
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate              # Hibernate schema yaratmaydi, faqat tekshiradi
```

```yaml
spring:
  jackson:
    default-property-inclusion: non_null   # null qiymatlar JSON javobda chiqmaydi
    serialization:
      write-dates-as-timestamps: false      # Sana "2026-03-25T10:00:00Z" formatda
```

```yaml
app:
  jwt:
    secret: ...                       # JWT imzolash kaliti (min 64 belgi)
    access-token-expiration: 900      # Access token: 15 daqiqa (900 soniya)
    refresh-token-expiration: 604800  # Refresh token: 7 kun (604800 soniya)
  minio:
    bucket-name: imvarxiv-documents   # Fayl saqlash uchun bucket nomi
```

### MUHIM: `open-in-view: false` nima demak?

Hibernate ma'lumotlarni "lazy" (kerak bo'lganda) yuklaydi. `open-in-view: true` bo'lsa, HTTP so'rov davomida DB ulanish ochiq turardi — bu resurs isrofgarchilik. `false` qilib qo'ydik, ya'ni:

```
Service metodi @Transactional → DB ulanish ochiladi → metod tugaydi → DB ulanish yopiladi
```

Bu qoida buzilsa: Service `@Transactional` bo'lmasa va lazy field ga kirishga harakat qilsa → **LazyInitializationException** xatosi chiqadi. Bu bizda bir marta `AuthService.getCurrentUser` da sodir bo'ldi — fix: `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` qo'shildi.

---

## 5. Ma'lumotlar Bazasi

### Flyway — Schema Versiyalash

Flyway har gal dastur ishlaganda `db/migration/` papkasidagi SQL fayllarni tekshiradi va yangilarini bajaradi.

```
V1__init_departments_users.sql    ← Birinchi bajariladi
V2__documents.sql
V3__approval_requests.sql
V4__nomenclature.sql
V5__journals_audit.sql
...
V8__...sql                        ← Eng oxirgi
```

**Qoida:** Eski migratsiya fayllari **hech qachon o'zgartirilmaydi** — yangi o'zgarish uchun yangi fayl yaratiladi (`V9__...sql`).

# IMV Arxiv — Arxitektura Qarorlari

**Versiya:** 1.0 | **Sana:** 2026-03-19

---

## 1. Arxitektura Uslubi: Pragmatik Modular Monolith

### Qaror
Microservices emas, **modular monolith** ishlatiladi.

### Sabab
- Loyiha birinchi versiyasi — domain chegaralari hali to'liq aniq emas
- Kichik jamoa (1-2 backend developer)
- Government loyiha — deployment soddaligi muhim
- Kelajakda kerak bo'lsa modullarni extract qilish mumkin

### Modul chegaralari
```
auth/       — Autentifikatsiya va avtorizatsiya
user/       — Foydalanuvchilar va boshqarmalar
document/   — Arxiv hujjatlar
request/    — Ariza va approval workflow
nomenclature/ — Nomenklatura
journal/    — Qabul va foydalanish jurnallari
report/     — Hisobotlar
shared/     — Utility va cross-cutting concerns
```

**Qoida:** Modullar faqat `UUID` (ID) orqali muloqot qiladi. Entity import qilinmaydi.

```kotlin
// TO'G'RI — faqat ID
class RequestService(...) {
    fun assign(requestId: UUID, documentId: UUID) { ... }
}

// NOTO'G'RI — Document entity import
class RequestService(private val documentRepository: DocumentRepository) { ... }
```

---

## 2. Layered Architecture (har modul ichida)

```
module/
├── api/              ← Controller — HTTP in/out, DTO validation
├── application/      ← Service — biznes logika, @Transactional
│   └── dto/          ← Request/Response DTO lar
├── domain/           ← Entity, Enum, Value Object lar
└── infrastructure/   ← Repository, external client lar
```

**Ma'lumot oqimi:**
```
HTTP Request → Controller → Service → Repository → Database
HTTP Response ← Controller ← Service ← Repository ← Database
                   ↑ DTO         ↑ Entity     ↑ JPA
```

---

## 3. Security Arxitekturasi

### JWT Stateless Authentication

```
Client → [Authorization: Bearer JWT] → JwtAuthenticationFilter
                                              ↓
                                    jwtTokenProvider.validateToken()
                                              ↓
                                    SecurityContextHolder.set(principal)
                                              ↓
                                    Controller → @CurrentUser principal
```

### Access Token vs Refresh Token

| Xususiyat | Access Token | Refresh Token |
|---|---|---|
| Muddat | 15 daqiqa | 7 kun |
| Saqlash (client) | Memory (Pinia store) | httpOnly Cookie |
| Saqlash (server) | Saqlanmaydi (stateless) | SHA-256 hash DB da |
| Revocation | Mumkin emas (muddat o'tguncha) | Mumkin (DB da revoke) |
| Claims | sub, userId, email, role, type | sub, jti, type |

### OneID OAuth2 Flow

```
1. Client → GET /auth/oneid/login → {authorizationUrl}
2. Client → Browser: redirect to OneID SSO
3. OneID → GET /auth/callback?code=X&state=Y
4. Server → POST OneID token endpoint (code exchange)
5. Server → GET OneID userinfo endpoint
6. Server → findOrCreate User → generate JWT → return {accessToken, refreshToken}
```

**State/Nonce:** CSRF va replay attack oldini olish uchun. Caffeine cache (5 daqiqa TTL). Distributed deployment da Redis ga o'tish kerak.

---

## 4. Database Arxitekturasi

### Flyway + Validate Mode

```
application.yml: spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: validate
```

**Sabab:** Hibernate DDL generation — prod da xavfli. Flyway migration lar aniq, versiyalangan, rollback mumkin.

**Qoida:** Migration fayllarni hech qachon o'zgartirma. Yangi migration qo'sh.

### Soft Delete

```sql
-- Schema da
is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT false

-- Hibernate da
@Where(clause = "is_deleted = false")
```

### Connection Pool (HikariCP)

```yaml
hikari:
  maximum-pool-size: 20
  minimum-idle: 5
  connection-timeout: 30000
  idle-timeout: 600000
  max-lifetime: 1800000
```

### N+1 Query Oldini Olish

```kotlin
// Option 1: @EntityGraph
@EntityGraph(attributePaths = ["department", "role"])
fun findAllWithDetails(): List<User>

// Option 2: JOIN FETCH
@Query("SELECT u FROM User u JOIN FETCH u.department WHERE u.isActive = true")
fun findAllActiveWithDepartment(): List<User>
```

---

## 5. Storage Arxitekturasi

### MinIO (S3-Compatible)

```
documents/{documentId}/pages/{pageNumber:03d}.jpg
```

**Presigned URL strategiyasi:**
- 15 daqiqalik muddat
- IP-bound (MinIO `ExtraQueryParams` orqali)
- Faqat `MODERATOR` va `APPROVER` roli ko'ra oladi
- Audit log har ko'rishda yoziladi

**Port va abstraction:**
```kotlin
interface StorageService { ... }           // Port (abstraction)
class MinioStorageService : StorageService { ... }  // Adapter (implementation)
```

Kelajakda AWS S3 ga o'tish uchun faqat yangi Adapter yoziladi.

---

## 6. Error Handling Strategiyasi

### Markazlashtirilgan Exception Handling

```
BusinessException(ErrorCode.USER_NOT_FOUND)
        ↓
GlobalExceptionHandler.handleBusinessException()
        ↓
ApiResponse.error(ErrorCode.USER_NOT_FOUND)
        ↓
HTTP 404 {"success":false,"error":{"code":"USER_NOT_FOUND"}}
```

**Frontend lokalizatsiyasi:** Backend faqat `code` qaytaradi. Frontend o'zbek tiliga tarjima qiladi.

```json
{
  "USER_NOT_FOUND": "Foydalanuvchi topilmadi",
  "DOCUMENT_NOT_COMPLETE": "Hujjat hali to'liq yuklanmagan"
}
```

---

## 7. Logging Strategiyasi

### MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context)

```kotlin
// RequestLoggingFilter da set:
MDC.put(MdcKeys.REQUEST_ID, requestId)
MDC.put(MdcKeys.USER_ID, userId)

// Har log entry da avtomatik:
log.info("User created")
// → {"requestId":"abc123","userId":"uuid","message":"User created","level":"INFO",...}
```

### Log Levels

| Level | Qachon |
|---|---|
| `ERROR` | Kutilmagan exception, external service down |
| `WARN` | Biznes validatsiya xatosi, token invalid |
| `INFO` | Muhim biznes event (login, document uploaded, request approved) |
| `DEBUG` | Faqat dev muhitida — SQL, HTTP calls |

server:
  port: 8080
  shutdown: graceful
  forward-headers-strategy: framework
  servlet:
    context-path: /api/v1

spring:
  application:
    name: imv-arxiv
  servlet:
    multipart:
      max-file-size: ${UPLOAD_MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB:300}MB
      max-request-size: 310MB
  profiles:
    active: ${SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE:dev}
  lifecycle:
    timeout-per-shutdown-phase: 30s

  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:5435}/${DB_NAME:imvarxiv}
    username: ${DB_USERNAME:imvarxiv}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD:imvarxiv_pass}
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 20
      minimum-idle: 5
      connection-timeout: 30000
      idle-timeout: 600000
      max-lifetime: 1800000
      pool-name: ImvArxivHikariPool

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    open-in-view: false
    show-sql: false
    properties:
      hibernate:
        format_sql: false
        jdbc:
          lob:
            non_contextual_creation: true

  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration
    baseline-on-migrate: false
    validate-on-migrate: true
    out-of-order: false

  jackson:
    default-property-inclusion: non_null
    serialization:
      write-dates-as-timestamps: false
    deserialization:
      fail-on-unknown-properties: false
    time-zone: UTC

  mvc:
    throw-exception-if-no-handler-found: true

  web:
    resources:
      add-mappings: false

app:
  jwt:
    secret: ${JWT_SECRET:dev-secret-key-minimum-64-characters-required-for-hs512-algorithm-use-prod-key}
    access-token-expiration: ${JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:900}
    refresh-token-expiration: ${JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:604800}

  minio:
    endpoint: ${MINIO_ENDPOINT:http://localhost:9000}
    access-key: ${MINIO_ACCESS_KEY:minioadmin}
    secret-key: ${MINIO_SECRET_KEY:minioadmin123}
    bucket-name: ${MINIO_BUCKET:imvarxiv-documents}

  sso:
    base-url: ${SSO_BASE_URL:http://localhost:9090}
    client-id: ${SSO_CLIENT_ID:test-client}
    client-secret: ${SSO_CLIENT_SECRET:test-secret}
    # Set to true only in dev/mock environments where SSO uses HMAC (no JWKS available)
    skip-id-token-verification: ${SSO_SKIP_ID_TOKEN_VERIFICATION:false}

  cors:
    allowed-origins: ${APP_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:http://localhost:3000,http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:5174}

management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health
      base-path: /actuator
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: never
      show-components: never
  health:
    db:
      enabled: false

springdoc:
  api-docs:
    path: /v3/api-docs
  swagger-ui:
    path: /swagger-ui.html
    enabled: true
    operations-sorter: method
    tags-sorter: alpha
  show-actuator: false
app:
  sso:
    base-url: ${SSO_BASE_URL:https://sso.mf.uz}
    client-id: imv-archive
    client-secret: mpApXQC1gyTK6N1Nk27KDiu4xGewMV
    # Real SSO uses RSA (JWKS available) — verify signature even in dev
    skip-id-token-verification: false
  jwt:
    access-token-expiration: 86400  # dev: 24 soat

  jpa:
    show-sql: true
    properties:
      hibernate:
        format_sql: true

management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: always
      show-components: always

springdoc:
  swagger-ui:
    enabled: true

  level:
    uz.gov.imvarxiv: DEBUG
    org.springframework.security: DEBUG

    org.flywaydb: INFO
    com.zaxxer.hikari: INFO

## 1. Project Overview

**What is this?** A government archive system (IMV Arxiv) for Uzbekistan. It digitizes paper archive documents — staff upload scanned JPEG pages, track document status, and control access via approval workflows.

**What the backend does:**
- Authenticates users via OneID (Uzbekistan government SSO)
- Manages archive documents: create → upload pages → complete → archive
- Controls what each role can do (SUPERADMIN / MODERATOR / APPROVER / USER)
- Stores document page images in MinIO (S3-compatible object storage)
- Generates presigned URLs so clients can view images directly from MinIO
- Tracks approval requests for accessing documents

**Running locally:**
```
PostgreSQL  → localhost:5435
MinIO       → localhost:9000
App         → localhost:8080/api/v1
Swagger UI  → http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui.html
```

## 4. Request Lifecycle

This is what happens from the moment a client sends `POST /api/v1/documents` until they get a response.

```
Client
  │
  │  POST /api/v1/documents
  │  Authorization: Bearer eyJ...
  │
  ▼
[RequestLoggingFilter]
  │  Assigns requestId (UUID), logs "→ POST /documents", starts timer
  │  Stores requestId in MDC (like Go's context.WithValue)
  ▼
[JwtAuthenticationFilter]
  │  Extracts "Bearer eyJ..." from Authorization header
  │  Calls JwtTokenProvider.validateToken()
  │  If valid → extracts oneidSub → loads UserPrincipal from DB
  │  Sets UserPrincipal in SecurityContext (available via @CurrentUser)
  │  If invalid → continues (request will fail at authorization step)
  ▼
[SecurityConfig route check]
  │  Is this route in the permitAll() list? → skip auth check
  │  Otherwise → is there a valid UserPrincipal? → if not → 401
  ▼
[DocumentController.createDraft()]
  │  @PreAuthorize("hasRole('MODERATOR')") → checks role → if wrong → 403
  │  @Valid @RequestBody → validates JSON fields → if invalid → 400 + field errors
  │  Calls DocumentService.createDraft(request, principal.id)
  ▼
[DocumentService.createDraft()]
  │  @Transactional → Spring opens a DB transaction automatically
  │  Creates Document entity, calls documentRepository.save(document)
  │  Hibernate translates this to: INSERT INTO documents (...)
  │  Returns DocumentDto (NOT the entity — converted before leaving service)
  │  @Transactional ends → Spring commits the transaction automatically
  ▼
[DocumentController]
  │  Wraps result in ApiResponse.success(dto)
  │  Returns ResponseEntity with HTTP 201
  ▼
[GlobalExceptionHandler] ← only if an exception was thrown anywhere above
  │  Catches BusinessException → formats error JSON → returns proper HTTP status
  ▼
[RequestLoggingFilter]
  │  Logs "← 201 POST /documents 45ms"
  ▼
Client receives:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { "id": "...", "status": "DRAFT", ... }
}
```

## 5. Core Patterns

### 5.1 Entity — Database Model

An entity is a Kotlin class that maps directly to a database table. Annotated with `@Entity`.

```kotlin
@Entity
@Table(name = "documents")          // which table in PostgreSQL
class Document(

    @Column(name = "title", nullable = false, length = 500)
    var title: String,              // maps to column "title"

    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    @Column(name = "status", length = 20)
    var status: DocumentStatus = DocumentStatus.DRAFT,

    // UUID of the user — NOT a join to User entity (modular monolith rule)
    @Column(name = "uploaded_by")
    val uploadedBy: UUID? = null,

) : BaseEntity()                    // inherits: id, createdAt, updatedAt
```

**`BaseEntity`** is extended by entities that have `id`, `created_at`, and `updated_at` columns.
If the DB table doesn't have `updated_at` — don't extend BaseEntity, define `@Id` manually.

```kotlin
// BaseEntity gives you these automatically:
val id: UUID = UUID.randomUUID()    // auto-generated UUID primary key
var createdAt: Instant              // set on INSERT
var updatedAt: Instant              // updated on every UPDATE
```

**In Go terms:** An entity is like a struct that you pass to `sqlx.StructScan()`, but here the framework handles all the SQL automatically.

```yaml
    endpoint: ${MINIO_ENDPOINT}
    access-key: ${MINIO_ACCESS_KEY}
    secret-key: ${MINIO_SECRET_KEY}
    bucket-name: ${MINIO_BUCKET:imvarxiv-documents}
  oneid:
    client-id: ${ONEID_CLIENT_ID}
    client-secret: ${ONEID_CLIENT_SECRET}
    redirect-uri: ${ONEID_REDIRECT_URI}
    authorization-uri: ${ONEID_AUTHORIZATION_URI}
    token-uri: ${ONEID_TOKEN_URI}
    userinfo-uri: ${ONEID_USERINFO_URI}
  cors:
    allowed-origins: ${APP_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS}

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}/${DB_NAME}
    username: ${DB_USERNAME}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD}
```

---

## Muhim Eslatmalar

1. `.env` fayl hech qachon git ga commit qilinmasin (`.gitignore` da)
2. `.env.example` template sifatida git da saqlanadi (actual qiymatlar yo'q)
3. Prod da environment variable lar OS level da yoki Docker secrets orqali inject qilinadi
4. JWT_SECRET har environment uchun alohida va unique bo'lishi kerak
5. OneID `client_secret` — vazirlikdan olinadi, log ga chiqarilmaydi

import client from './client'
import type { ApiResponse } from '@/types/common'
import type { AuthResponse, Role, UserDto } from '@/types/auth'

export const authApi = {
  devLogin: (params: { role: Role; pinfl?: string; fullName?: string; departmentId?: string; departmentName?: string }) =>
    client.post<ApiResponse<AuthResponse>>('/auth/dev/login', params),

    client.get<ApiResponse<{ loginUrl: string }>>('/auth/login-url', {
      params: { codeChallenge, redirectUri },
    }),

  token: (body: {
    grantType: string
    code?: string
    codeVerifier?: string
    refreshToken?: string
    redirectUri?: string
  }) => client.post<ApiResponse<AuthResponse>>('/auth/token', body),

  logout: (refreshToken: string) =>
    client.post<ApiResponse<null>>('/auth/logout', { refreshToken }),

  me: () =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<UserDto>>('/auth/me'),
}
import client from './client'
import type { ApiResponse, PageResponse } from '@/types/common'
import type { DocumentDto, DocumentPageDto, CreateNomenklaturaRequest, CreateRoyxatlarRequest, ProcessingStatusDto } from '@/types/document'

export const documentsApi = {
  // Nomenklatura
  listNomenklatura: (params: { departmentId?: string; storagePeriod?: string; search?: string; page?: number; size?: number } = {}) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<PageResponse<DocumentDto>>>('/documents/nomenklatura', { params }),

  createNomenklatura: (data: CreateNomenklaturaRequest) =>
    client.post<ApiResponse<DocumentDto>>('/documents/nomenklatura', data),

  // Royxatlar
  listRoyxatlar: (params: { departmentId?: string; search?: string; page?: number; size?: number } = {}) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<PageResponse<DocumentDto>>>('/documents/royxatlar', { params }),

  createRoyxatlar: (data: CreateRoyxatlarRequest) =>
    client.post<ApiResponse<DocumentDto>>('/documents/royxatlar', data),

  getById: (documentId: string) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<DocumentDto>>(`/documents/${documentId}`),

  // Pages
  getPages: (documentId: string) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<DocumentPageDto[]>>(`/documents/${documentId}/pages`),

  uploadPages: (documentId: string, entries: { file: File; pageNumber: number }[]) => {
    const form = new FormData()
    entries.forEach(({ file }) => form.append('files', file))
    form.append('pageNumbers', entries.map(e => e.pageNumber).join(','))
    return client.post<ApiResponse<DocumentPageDto[]>>(`/documents/${documentId}/pages`, form, {
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data' },
    })
  },

  deletePage: (documentId: string, pageId: string) =>
    client.delete(`/documents/${documentId}/pages/${pageId}`),

  // PDF upload + processing status
  uploadPdf: (documentId: string, file: File, onProgress?: (pct: number) => void) => {
    const form = new FormData()
    form.append('file', file)
    return client.post<ApiResponse<ProcessingStatusDto>>(`/documents/${documentId}/upload-pdf`, form, {
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data' },
      onUploadProgress: (e) => {
        if (onProgress && e.total) onProgress(Math.round((e.loaded * 100) / e.total))
      },
    })
  },

  getProcessingStatus: (documentId: string) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<ProcessingStatusDto>>(`/documents/${documentId}/processing-status`),

  // Search across both types (queries nomenklatura + royxatlar with size=10)
  searchNomenklatura: (search: string) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<PageResponse<DocumentDto>>>('/documents/nomenklatura', { params: { search, size: 10 } }),

  searchRoyxatlar: (search: string) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<PageResponse<DocumentDto>>>('/documents/royxatlar', { params: { search, size: 10 } }),
}
import client from './client'
import type { ApiResponse, PageResponse } from '@/types/common'
import type {
  ApprovalRequestDto,
  RequestSentPageDto,
  RequestTemplateDto,
  RequestStatus,
  CreateRequestDto,
  ApproveRequestDto,
  RejectRequestDto,
  RevertRequestDto,
} from '@/types/request'

export const requestsApi = {
  // USER: create
  create: (data: CreateRequestDto) =>
    client.post<ApiResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>('/requests', data),

  // USER: own requests
  listMine: (params: { status?: RequestStatus; page?: number; size?: number } = {}) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<PageResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>>('/requests', { params }),

  // APPROVER: all requests (any status)
  listAll: (params: { status?: RequestStatus; page?: number; size?: number } = {}) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<PageResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>>('/requests/all', { params }),

  // MODERATOR: inbox (all requests, filterable by status; default = all)
  inbox: (params: { status?: RequestStatus | null; page?: number; size?: number } = {}) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<PageResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>>('/requests/inbox', { params }),

  getById: (id: string) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>(`/requests/${id}`),

  approve: (id: string, data: ApproveRequestDto) =>
    client.patch<ApiResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>(`/requests/${id}/approve`, data),

  reject: (id: string, data: RejectRequestDto) =>
    client.patch<ApiResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>(`/requests/${id}/reject`, data),

  revert: (id: string, data: RevertRequestDto) =>
    client.patch<ApiResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>(`/requests/${id}/revert`, data),

  // MODERATOR: send (attach document + selected page IDs in one step)
  send: (id: string, data: { documentId: string; pageIds: string[] }) =>
    client.patch<ApiResponse<ApprovalRequestDto>>(`/requests/${id}/send`, data),

  // Sent pages (USER views after SENT)
  getSentPages: (id: string) =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<RequestSentPageDto[]>>(`/requests/${id}/pages`),

  downloadZip: (id: string) =>
    client.get(`/requests/${id}/download/zip`, { responseType: 'blob' }),

  downloadPdf: (id: string) =>
    client.get(`/requests/${id}/download/pdf`, { responseType: 'blob' }),
}

export const templatesApi = {
  list: () =>
    client.get<ApiResponse<RequestTemplateDto[]>>('/request-templates'),

  create: (data: { name: string; content: string }) =>
    client.post<ApiResponse<RequestTemplateDto>>('/request-templates', data),

  update: (id: string, data: { name: string; content: string }) =>
    client.put<ApiResponse<RequestTemplateDto>>(`/request-templates/${id}`, data),

  delete: (id: string) =>
    client.delete<ApiResponse<null>>(`/request-templates/${id}`),
}

BACKEND_DIR := archive-backend-kotlin
FRONTEND_DIR := frontend
SHELL := /bin/zsh
export DOCKER_HOST := unix:///$(HOME)/.docker/run/docker.sock

.PHONY: dev run infra backend frontend stop clean logs setup help

## ─── Primary target ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

dev: run ## Alias for run

## ─── Setup (run once on new machine) ────────────────────────────────────────

setup: ## Remove macOS quarantine and fix permissions (run once after clone/copy)
	@echo "Removing macOS quarantine attributes..."
	@xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine . 2>/dev/null || true
	@chmod +x $(BACKEND_DIR)/gradlew
	@if [ -d "$(FRONTEND_DIR)/node_modules" ]; then \
		find $(FRONTEND_DIR)/node_modules/.bin -type f | xargs chmod +x 2>/dev/null || true; \
	fi
	@echo "  Done. You can now run: make dev"

## ─── Individual targets ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

infra: ## Start Docker infra (postgres, minio)
	@echo "Starting Docker services..."
	@cd $(BACKEND_DIR) && docker compose up -d postgres minio minio-init
	@echo "Waiting for PostgreSQL..."
	@until docker exec imvarxiv-postgres pg_isready -U imvarxiv -d imvarxiv > /dev/null 2>&1; do \
		printf '.'; sleep 1; \
	done
	@echo ""
	@echo "  PostgreSQL ready  → localhost:5435"
	@echo "  MinIO ready       → http://localhost:9000  (console: http://localhost:9001)"

backend: ## Build and run Spring Boot only (infra must already be running)
	cd $(BACKEND_DIR) && ./gradlew bootRun

frontend: ## Run Vite dev server only
	cd $(FRONTEND_DIR) && npm run dev

stop: ## Stop Docker infra containers (backend/frontend must be killed manually)
	cd $(BACKEND_DIR) && docker compose stop

clean: ## Stop containers and wipe volumes (data loss!)
	cd $(BACKEND_DIR) && docker compose down -v

logs: ## Tail Docker container logs
	cd $(BACKEND_DIR) && docker compose logs -f

## ─── Internal ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Cross-platform local IP detection
LOCAL_IP := $(shell ipconfig getifaddr en0 2>/dev/null || ipconfig getifaddr en1 2>/dev/null || hostname -I 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $$1}' || echo "localhost")

# Cross-platform port kill (macOS: lsof, Linux: fuser)
define kill_port
	@lsof -ti:$(1) 2>/dev/null | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || fuser -k $(1)/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
endef

run: infra ## Start everything: infra + backend + frontend
	$(call kill_port,8080)
	@cd $(FRONTEND_DIR) && if [ ! -f .npm_installed ] || [ package.json -nt .npm_installed ]; then \
		echo "Installing frontend dependencies..."; \
		rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json && npm install --silent && touch .npm_installed; \
	fi
	@trap 'echo ""; echo "Stopping..."; kill $$BACKEND_PID $$FRONTEND_PID 2>/dev/null; wait $$BACKEND_PID $$FRONTEND_PID 2>/dev/null; exit 0' INT TERM; \
	( cd $(BACKEND_DIR) && ./gradlew bootRun 2>&1 | tee ../backend.log ) & BACKEND_PID=$$!; \
	( cd $(FRONTEND_DIR) && npm run dev 2>&1 | tee ../frontend.log ) & FRONTEND_PID=$$!; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "  Backend  → http://localhost:8080/api/v1"; \
	echo "  Frontend → http://localhost:5173"; \
	echo "  Network  → http://$(LOCAL_IP):5173  (share this with frontend dev)"; \
	echo "  Swagger  → http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui.html"; \
	echo "  Dev login→ POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/dev/login"; \
	echo ""; \
	wait $$BACKEND_PID $$FRONTEND_PID

## ─── Help ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

help: ## Show this help
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*## ' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | \
		awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*## "}; {printf "  \033[36m%-12s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2}'

.DEFAULT_GOAL := help
services:

  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: imvarxiv-postgres
    ports:
      - "5435:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-imvarxiv}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-imvarxiv}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-imvarxiv_pass}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-imvarxiv} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-imvarxiv}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    networks:
      - imvarxiv-network
    restart: unless-stopped

  minio:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    container_name: imvarxiv-minio
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
      - "9001:9001"
    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: ${MINIO_ROOT_USER:-minioadmin}
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD:-minioadmin123}
    volumes:
      - minio_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:9000/minio/health/live"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 15s
    networks:
      - imvarxiv-network
    restart: unless-stopped

  minio-init:
    image: minio/mc:latest
    container_name: imvarxiv-minio-init
    depends_on:
      minio:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: ${MINIO_ROOT_USER:-minioadmin}
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD:-minioadmin123}
    entrypoint: >
      /bin/sh -c "
        mc alias set local http://minio:9000 $$MINIO_ROOT_USER $$MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD &&
        mc mb --ignore-existing local/imvarxiv-documents &&
        mc anonymous set private local/imvarxiv-documents &&
        echo 'MinIO initialized: bucket imvarxiv-documents created'
      "
    networks:
      - imvarxiv-network

  mock-sso:
    build: ../mock-sso
    container_name: imvarxiv-mock-sso
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"
    networks:
      - imvarxiv-network
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  postgres_data:
    name: imvarxiv-postgres-data
  minio_data:
    name: imvarxiv-minio-data

networks:
  imvarxiv-network:
    name: imvarxiv-network
    driver: bridge
# App-only compose — Staging/Prod server deployment uchun
# Infrastructure (Postgres, MinIO) alohida manage qilinadi
#
# Ishlatish (DevOps server da):
#   IMAGE_TAG=abc123 CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE=registry.gitlab.com/org/repo \
#     docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d
#
# Yoki .env fayliga yozilsa:
#   docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d

services:
  app:
    image: ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: imvarxiv-app
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${APP_PORT:-8080}:8080"
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE:         ${SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE}
      # ── Database ─────────────────────────────────
      DB_HOST:                         ${DB_HOST}
      DB_PORT:                         ${DB_PORT:-5432}
      DB_NAME:                         ${DB_NAME}
      DB_USERNAME:                     ${DB_USERNAME}
      DB_PASSWORD:                     ${DB_PASSWORD}
      # ── JWT ──────────────────────────────────────
      JWT_SECRET:                      ${JWT_SECRET}
      JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:   ${JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:-900}
      JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:  ${JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:-604800}
      # ── MinIO ────────────────────────────────────
      MINIO_ENDPOINT:                  ${MINIO_ENDPOINT}
      MINIO_ACCESS_KEY:                ${MINIO_ACCESS_KEY}
      MINIO_SECRET_KEY:                ${MINIO_SECRET_KEY}
      MINIO_BUCKET:                    ${MINIO_BUCKET:-imvarxiv-documents}
      # ── SSO ──────────────────────────────────────
      SSO_BASE_URL:                    ${SSO_BASE_URL}
      SSO_CLIENT_ID:                   ${SSO_CLIENT_ID}
      SSO_CLIENT_SECRET:               ${SSO_CLIENT_SECRET}
      # ── CORS ─────────────────────────────────────
      APP_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:        ${APP_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS}
    networks:
      - imvarxiv-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 60s
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "50m"
        max-file: "5"

networks:
  imvarxiv-network:
    external: true
    name: ${DOCKER_NETWORK:-imvarxiv-network}

#   IMAGE_TAG=abc123 CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE=registry.gitlab.com/org/repo \
#     docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d
#
# Yoki .env fayliga yozilsa:
#   docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d

services:
  app:
    image: ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: imvarxiv-app
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${APP_PORT:-8080}:8080"
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE:         ${SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE}
      # ── Database ─────────────────────────────────
      DB_HOST:                         ${DB_HOST}
      DB_PORT:                         ${DB_PORT:-5432}
      DB_NAME:                         ${DB_NAME}
      DB_USERNAME:                     ${DB_USERNAME}
      DB_PASSWORD:                     ${DB_PASSWORD}
      # ── JWT ──────────────────────────────────────
      JWT_SECRET:                      ${JWT_SECRET}
      JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:   ${JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:-900}
      JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:  ${JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_SECONDS:-604800}
      # ── MinIO ────────────────────────────────────
      MINIO_ENDPOINT:                  ${MINIO_ENDPOINT}
      MINIO_ACCESS_KEY:                ${MINIO_ACCESS_KEY}
      MINIO_SECRET_KEY:                ${MINIO_SECRET_KEY}
      MINIO_BUCKET:                    ${MINIO_BUCKET:-imvarxiv-documents}
      # ── SSO ──────────────────────────────────────
      SSO_BASE_URL:                    ${SSO_BASE_URL}
      SSO_CLIENT_ID:                   ${SSO_CLIENT_ID}
      SSO_CLIENT_SECRET:               ${SSO_CLIENT_SECRET}
      # ── CORS ─────────────────────────────────────
      APP_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:        ${APP_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS}
    networks:
      - imvarxiv-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 60s
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "50m"
        max-file: "5"

networks:
  imvarxiv-network:
    external: true
    name: ${DOCKER_NETWORK:-imvarxiv-network}

payload.message:
I studied the IMV archive project in detail. It’s a three-part app:

- Backend: Kotlin 1.9 / Spring Boot 3.3 modular monolith in [archive-backend-kotlin](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin)
- Frontend: Vue 3 / Vite / Pinia / Element Plus in [frontend](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/frontend)
- Local mock SSO: Node/Express in [mock-sso](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/mock-sso)

The intended domain is clear: IMV archive users create requests for archive document pages, approvers approve/reject, moderators attach selected document pages, and users download/view the approved pages. Backend modules are organized as `auth`, `user`, `document`, `request`, `fond`, `report`, and `shared`, with Flyway migrations up to `V22`.

Key findings:

- Backend currently does **not compile**. `./gradlew test` fails because of a stray character in [AuthController.kt](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin/src/main/kotlin/uz/gov/imvarxiv/auth/api/AuthController.kt):120 after `@field:NotBlank`.
- Frontend production build passes: `npm run build` completed successfully.
- Frontend calls some backend routes that do not exist:
  - [requests.ts](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/frontend/src/api/requests.ts):40 calls `PATCH /requests/{id}/revert`, but [RequestController.kt](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin/src/main/kotlin/uz/gov/imvarxiv/request/api/RequestController.kt):75 only has approve/reject/send/download.
  - [requests.ts](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/frontend/src/api/requests.ts):48 calls `GET /requests/{id}/pages`, but backend exposes ZIP/PDF download, not that endpoint.
  - [requests.ts](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/frontend/src/api/requests.ts):65 uses `PUT /request-templates/{id}`, but backend uses `PATCH` in [RequestTemplateController.kt](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin/src/main/kotlin/uz/gov/imvarxiv/request/api/RequestTemplateController.kt):39.
- Reports are only stubs: [ReportService.kt](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin/src/main/kotlin/uz/gov/imvarxiv/report/application/ReportService.kt):8 returns empty data.
- Deployment healthcheck likely points to the wrong path: [docker-compose.app.yml](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin/docker-compose.app.yml):44 checks `/actuator/health`, but the app has context path `/api/v1` in [application.yml](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin/src/main/resources/application.yml):6, so local docs elsewhere use `/api/v1/actuator/health`.
- There is a hardcoded SSO client secret in dev config at [application-dev.yml](/Users/sam21/codebase/gitlab.mf.uz/archive/archive-backend-kotlin/src/main/resources/application-dev.yml):5. Even if dev-only, I’d rotate/remove it if this repo is shared.
- There are no normal test files found under backend/frontend test locations, so current verification is mostly compile/build rather than meaningful coverage.

Overall: the architecture is understandable and mostly consistent, but the backend compile error is the first blocker. After that, I’d align frontend/backend request/template endpoints, then implement/report-stub cleanup, then add smoke tests around auth, request workflow, and document page upload.
